### Assistant
> Here’s the blunt truth:
> 
> Your structure is **trying to do 4 jobs at once**:
> 
> - working case management
> - evidence vault
> - writing/drafting studio
> - archive/export dump
> 
> That is why it feels cursed.
> 
> ## The fix
> 
> Stop organizing by “whatever the file feels like.”  
> Organize by **function**.
> 
> You only need **7 top-level buckets** for this case.
> 
> # Final structure
> 
> ```text
> FCFCU_Case/
> ├── 0_Case_Ops/
> │   ├── 00_Dashboard/
> │   ├── 01_Facts/
> │   │   ├── Actors/
> │   │   ├── Events/
> │   │   ├── Issues/
> │   │   ├── Damages/
> │   │   ├── Docket/
> │   │   └── Deadlines/
> │   ├── 02_Strategy/
> │   │   ├── Phase_Maps/
> │   │   ├── Hearing_Prep/
> │   │   ├── Settlement/
> │   │   ├── Regulatory/
> │   │   └── Attorney_Intake/
> │   ├── 03_Pleadings/
> │   │   ├── Court_Filed/
> │   │   ├── Court_Drafts/
> │   │   ├── Complaints_Filed/
> │   │   ├── Complaints_Drafts/
> │   │   ├── Notices/
> │   │   ├── Orders/
> │   │   ├── Affidavits/
> │   │   ├── Declarations/
> │   │   └── Exhibits_Working/
> │   └── 04_Reference/
> │       ├── Law/
> │       ├── Agency/
> │       ├── Research/
> │       └── Templates/
> │
> ├── 1_Evidence/
> │   ├── 01_Documents/
> │   │   ├── Court/
> │   │   ├── FCFCU/
> │   │   ├── Credit/
> │   │   ├── Utilities/
> │   │   ├── Property/
> │   │   ├── Mailings/
> │   │   ├── Email/
> │   │   ├── Financial/
> │   │   └── Misc/
> │   ├── 02_Photos/
> │   ├── 03_Video/
> │   ├── 04_Audio/
> │   ├── 05_Transcripts/
> │   ├── 06_Exhibits_Final/
> │   └── 99_Retained_Raw/
> │
> ├── 2_Data/
> │   ├── json/
> │   ├── csv/
> │   ├── manifests/
> │   ├── imports/
> │   └── exports/
> │
> ├── 3_Reports/
> │   ├── Audits/
> │   ├── Analysis/
> │   ├── Summaries/
> │   └── Timelines/
> │
> ├── 4_Governance/
> │   ├── Naming_Rules/
> │   ├── Schema/
> │   ├── Migration/
> │   ├── Classification/
> │   └── SOPs/
> │
> ├── 5_Scripts/
> │   ├── ingest/
> │   ├── normalize/
> │   ├── export/
> │   ├── maintenance/
> │   └── archive/
> │
> └── 9_Archive/
>     ├── Legacy_Dumps/
>     ├── OCR_Temp/
>     ├── Auto_Saves/
>     ├── BAK/
>     └── Quarantine/
> ```
> 
> ## What goes where
> 
> ## 0_Case_Ops
> This is your **live working brain**.
> 
> Only:
> - facts
> - strategy
> - pleadings
> - references
> 
> This should be readable by you and eventually an attorney.
> 
> Your current:
> - `1_FACTS`
> - `1_Strategy`
> - `5_Pleadings`
> - most of `0_Case_Map/_ref`
> belong here.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## 1_Evidence
> This is the **actual evidence vault**.
> 
> Only source material and final exhibit material.
> 
> Your current:
> - `2_Evidence`
> - `_pdf`
> - `_imgs`
> - `_vid`
> - `_eml`
> - `_txt`
> - `_notices`
> - a lot of `2_docs`
> belong here.
> 
> This folder should not be full of strategy notes and random draft markdown.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## 2_Data
> This is for structured machine-readable material only.
> 
> Only:
> - CSVs
> - JSON
> - manifests
> - import packs
> - export packs
> 
> Your current:
> - `4_meta/data`
> - `_sheets`
> - `_reports.csv`
> - `_docs.csv`
> - `_docket.csv`
> - template CSVs
> - JSON manifests
> belong here.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## 3_Reports
> This is for generated outputs.
> 
> Only:
> - audit reports
> - AI reports
> - timeline exports
> - transcript exports
> - analysis PDFs
> 
> Your current:
> - `3_reports`
> - `5_exports/analysis`
> - transcript export bundles
> belong here.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## 4_Governance
> This is where repo rules live.
> 
> Only:
> - structure rules
> - naming rules
> - schema rules
> - field maps
> - migration instructions
> 
> Your current:
> - `governance`
> - `4_meta/LITIGATION_SCHEMA_LOCK.md`
> - schema headers
> - frontmatter schema files
> belong here.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## 5_Scripts
> Only scripts and script support.
> 
> Your current `6_scripts` stays, but:
> - `_tmp_ocr` does **not** belong as live content
> - generated dump text does **not** belong mixed into script folder
> 
> Move those to `9_Archive`.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## 9_Archive
> This is where junk goes that you **must retain but do not want polluting the active repo**.
> 
> Examples:
> - `_tmp_ocr`
> - `.bak`
> - giant raw tree dumps
> - duplicate OCR jpgs generated from PDFs
> - auto-save garbage
> - weird one-off files like `dfadadf.pdf`
> 
> Do not delete first. Quarantine first.
> 
> # What to kill immediately
> 
> These are the patterns wrecking your structure:
> 
> ## 1. underscore pseudo-folders everywhere
> Stuff like:
> - `_pdf`
> - `_md`
> - `_imgs`
> - `_txt`
> - `_drafts`
> 
> This is not structure. It’s a panic drawer.
> 
> Replace with actual functional locations.
> 
> ## 2. same file in 4 places
> You’ve got docs showing up in:
> - `content/0_Case_Map/...`
> - `2_docs`
> - `2_Evidence`
> - `_tmp_ocr`
> - sometimes image/PDF/txt variants all mixed
> 
> That is fine for retention, but not fine for navigation.
> 
> Rule:
> - **one canonical working location**
> - **one evidence source location**
> - everything else is derivative/archive
> 
> ## 3. strategy mixed with evidence
> Bad.
> A demand draft is not evidence.
> An outline is not evidence.
> A packet HTML file is not evidence.
> 
> ## 4. OCR output treated like evidence
> Wrong.
> OCR text is derivative, not source evidence.
> 
> OCR belongs in:
> - `1_Evidence/05_Transcripts/` if cleaned and intentional
> - or `9_Archive/OCR_Temp/` if machine-generated junk
> 
> ## 5. gigantic video scatter
> Your video structure is too granular in some places and too duplicated in others.
> 
> You need:
> 
> ```text
> 1_Evidence/03_Video/
> ├── 2025-11-26_Sheriff_No_Notice/
> ├── 2025-12-03_Police_Knock_Leave/
> ├── 2025-12-09_Removal_Day/
> │   ├── Bedroom_Camera/
> │   ├── Garage_Camera/
> │   ├── Porch_Camera/
> │   ├── Hallway_Camera/
> │   ├── Kitchen_Camera/
> │   └── Photos/
> ├── 2025-12-14_Porch_Camera/
> ├── 2025-12-17_Retrieval_Day/
> │   ├── Bedroom_Camera/
> │   ├── Driveway_Camera/
> │   └── Photos/
> └── 2026-01-09_Hearing_Recording/
> ```
> 
> That’s enough. Stop nesting by every hour unless you truly need it.
> 
> # The actual mapping from what you have now
> 
> ## Move these into `0_Case_Ops/01_Facts`
> - `1_FACTS/ACTORS`
> - `1_FACTS/DAMAGES`
> - `1_FACTS/DOCKET`
> - `1_FACTS/EVENTS`
> - `1_FACTS/ISSUES`
> 
> ## Move these into `0_Case_Ops/02_Strategy`
> - `1_Strategy/*`
> - strategic memos from `_md`
> - valuation memos
> - settlement plans
> - oral argument outlines
> - question matrices
> 
> ## Move these into `0_Case_Ops/03_Pleadings`
> - `5_Pleadings/*`
> - court-ready motions
> - objections
> - notices
> - declarations
> - affidavits
> - proposed orders
> - complaint packets
> 
> ## Move these into `0_Case_Ops/04_Reference`
> - `_ref/*`
> - legal standards
> - agency references
> - research precedent CSV
> - templates that are actually reusable
> 
> ## Move these into `1_Evidence/01_Documents`
> - `2_docs/*` except templates/deadline notes
> - `2_Evidence/EV-DOCS/*`
> - source PDFs from `_pdf`
> - source emails from `_eml`
> - source notices from `_notices`
> 
> ## Move these into `1_Evidence/02_Photos`
> - `EV-PHOTOS/*`
> - source image evidence from `_imgs`
> - but not font files, favicon crap, website assets
> 
> ## Move these into `1_Evidence/03_Video`
> - `EV-VIDEO/*`
> - source camera files from `_vid`
> - not duplicate compressed junk unless it is the only copy
> 
> ## Move these into `1_Evidence/04_Audio`
> - hearing recordings
> - `.m4a`
> - voicemail audio
> - dictated case review audio if source
> 
> ## Move these into `1_Evidence/05_Transcripts`
> - cleaned transcript `.txt`
> - hearing transcript exports
> - OCR-derived transcript text if it is intentionally retained
> 
> ## Move these into `2_Data`
> - all CSV master files
> - JSON manifests
> - import/export structured files
> - sheet exports
> - database bootstrap packs
> 
> ## Move these into `9_Archive`
> - `_tmp_ocr`
> - `.bak`
> - duplicate jpg-from-pdf render sets
> - nonsense files
> - autogenerated dump text
> - stale autosaves
> - weird font/web asset junk
> 
> # The naming rule you should lock
> 
> ## Working docs
> Human-readable:
> ```text
> YYYY-MM-DD_short_title.ext
> ```
> 
> ## Canonical evidence
> Keep your coded names:
> ```text
> YYYY-MM-DD_C##_TYPE_SOURCE_D####.ext
> ```
> 
> ## Facts records
> Use object IDs:
> ```text
> ACT-...
> EVT-...
> ISS-...
> DMG-...
> FIL-...
> ```
> 
> ## Never mix
> Do not mix:
> - casual names
> - coded names
> - half-coded names
> - duplicate renamed variants
> 
> Pick one rule per object class.
> 
> # The simplest principle
> 
> Think in this stack:
> 
> ## Live work
> `0_Case_Ops`
> 
> ## Proof
> `1_Evidence`
> 
> ## Structured data
> `2_Data`
> 
> ## Outputs
> `3_Reports`
> 
> ## Rules
> `4_Governance`
> 
> ## Utilities
> `5_Scripts`
> 
> ## Graveyard
> `9_Archive`
> 
> That’s it.
> 
> # My strongest recommendation
> 
> Do **not** try to manually reorganize every file first.
> 
> Do this in order:
> 
> 1. create the final top-level structure
> 2. move only the **major folders** into their correct bucket
> 3. quarantine junk into `9_Archive`
> 4. stop there
> 5. then run a script-based normalization pass for filenames and duplicates
> 
> Because right now you do not need a perfect filing cabinet.  
> You need a structure that stops actively sabotaging retrieval.
> 
> # Bottom line
> 
> Your repo should be organized by:
> 
> - **operations**
> - **evidence**
> - **data**
> - **reports**
> - **governance**
> - **scripts**
> - **archive**
> 
> Not by:
> - format
> - mood
> - temporary use
> - random historical leftovers
> 
> This is the clean answer.
> 
> Next move is to turn this into a **move map**:
> `old path -> new path`, so you can feed it to the IDE or a script without guessing.

### Assistant
> Yep. Based on what you sent, the move is obvious:
> 
> **source**
> `g:\My Drive\QiVault\3_projects\3.3_cases\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\content\0_Case_Ops\_Intake\_txt`
> 
> **target**
> `g:\My Drive\QiVault\3_projects\3.3_cases\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\content\9_Archive\_txt`
> 
> So here’s a **safe Python script** that will:
> 
> - dry-run first
> - create missing archive folders
> - move all files from the source `_txt` folder to the archive `_txt` folder
> - skip if source file is missing
> - stop on collisions unless you explicitly allow overwrite
> - log everything
> 
> ## Script
> 
> ```python
> from pathlib import Path
> import shutil
> import sys
> 
> # =========================
> # CONFIG
> # =========================
> DRY_RUN = True          # change to False after reviewing output
> OVERWRITE = False       # change to True only if you explicitly want replacement
> 
> BASE = Path(r"g:\My Drive\QiVault\3_projects\3.3_cases\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\content")
> SOURCE_DIR = BASE / "0_Case_Ops" / "_Intake" / "_txt"
> TARGET_DIR = BASE / "9_Archive" / "_txt"
> 
> FILES = [
>     "1331-23-81_C_DOC_D0001.txt",
>     "1331-24-62_C_DOC_D0002.txt",
>     "1331-24-62_C_DOC_D0003.txt",
>     "1770-10-79_C_DOC_D0491.txt",
>     "2025-10-29_C_DOC_D0017.txt",
>     "2025-12-21_C_DOC_D0068.txt",
>     "2025-12-22_C_DOC_D0451.txt",
>     "2026-01-08_C_DOC_D0474.txt",
>     "2026-01-18_C_DOC_D0475.txt",
>     "2026-01-18_C_DOC_D0478.txt",
>     "2026-01-18_C_DOC_D0482.txt",
>     "2026-01-24_C_DOC_D0488.txt",
>     "2026-02-03_C_DOC_D0476.txt",
>     "2026-02-03_C_DOC_D0483.txt",
>     "2026-02-03_C_DOC_D0492.txt",
>     "2026-02-03_C_DOC_D0493.txt",
>     "2026-02-03_C_DOC_D0498.txt",
>     "2026-02-03_C_DOC_D0499.txt",
>     "2026-02-03_C_DOC_D0500.txt",
>     "2026-02-03_C_DOC_D0501.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0464.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0467.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0468.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0470.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0471.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0472.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0479.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0485.txt",
>     "2026-02-07_C_DOC_D0495.txt",
>     "6018-56-13_C_DOC_D0480.txt",
>     "FCFCU_Late_Reporting_Evidence_2026.txt",
>     "FINANCIAL_CENTER_01012017-02122026.txt",
>     "_txt.md",
>     "audit_report.txt",
>     "output.txt",
> ]
> 
> # =========================
> # LOGIC
> # =========================
> def move_file(src: Path, dst: Path):
>     if not src.exists():
>         print(f"[MISSING] {src}")
>         return
> 
>     if dst.exists():
>         if not OVERWRITE:
>             print(f"[COLLISION] Target exists, skipped: {dst}")
>             return
>         else:
>             print(f"[OVERWRITE] {dst}")
> 
>     print(f"[MOVE] {src} -> {dst}")
> 
>     if not DRY_RUN:
>         dst.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
>         if OVERWRITE and dst.exists():
>             dst.unlink()
>         shutil.move(str(src), str(dst))
> 
> 
> def main():
>     print("=" * 80)
>     print("TXT ARCHIVE MOVE")
>     print(f"DRY_RUN   = {DRY_RUN}")
>     print(f"OVERWRITE = {OVERWRITE}")
>     print(f"SOURCE    = {SOURCE_DIR}")
>     print(f"TARGET    = {TARGET_DIR}")
>     print("=" * 80)
> 
>     if not SOURCE_DIR.exists():
>         print(f"[ERROR] Source directory does not exist: {SOURCE_DIR}")
>         sys.exit(1)
> 
>     if not DRY_RUN:
>         TARGET_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
> 
>     for name in FILES:
>         src = SOURCE_DIR / name
>         dst = TARGET_DIR / name
>         move_file(src, dst)
> 
>     print("=" * 80)
>     print("DONE")
>     print("=" * 80)
> 
> 
> if __name__ == "__main__":
>     main()
> ```
> 
> ## How to use it
> 
> Save as something like:
> 
> ```powershell
> g:\My Drive\QiVault\3_projects\3.3_cases\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\content\5_Scripts\archive_txt_files.py
> ```
> 
> Run it:

### Assistant
> Yep. That’s the right move.
> 
> Stop hand-editing chaos. First generate a **full file manifest CSV** from the vault, then use that CSV as the rename/move control sheet.
> 
> Use this script as `5_Scripts\build_content_manifest.py`.
> 
> ```python
> from pathlib import Path
> import csv
> from datetime import datetime
> import hashlib
> 
> # ============================================================
> # CONFIG
> # ============================================================
> 
> ROOT = Path(r"g:\My Drive\QiVault\3_projects\3.3_cases\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\content")
> OUTPUT_DIR = ROOT / "2_Data" / "manifests"
> TIMESTAMP = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
> OUTPUT_CSV = OUTPUT_DIR / f"content_manifest_{TIMESTAMP}.csv"
> 
> INCLUDE_HASH = False       # True = slower, but gives sha256 for files
> HASH_CHUNK_SIZE = 1024 * 1024
> RELATIVE_TO_ROOT = True
> 
> # ============================================================
> # HELPERS
> # ============================================================
> 
> def sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:
>     h = hashlib.sha256()
>     with path.open("rb") as f:
>         while True:
>             chunk = f.read(HASH_CHUNK_SIZE)
>             if not chunk:
>                 break
>             h.update(chunk)
>     return h.hexdigest()
> 
> def safe_stat(path: Path):
>     try:
>         return path.stat()
>     except Exception:
>         return None
> 
> def path_to_str(path: Path) -> str:
>     return str(path.relative_to(ROOT)) if RELATIVE_TO_ROOT else str(path)
> 
> def get_depth(path: Path) -> int:
>     rel = path.relative_to(ROOT)
>     return len(rel.parts)
> 
> def get_parent(path: Path) -> str:
>     if path == ROOT:
>         return ""
>     parent = path.parent
>     return path_to_str(parent)
> 
> def get_extension(path: Path) -> str:
>     if path.is_dir():
>         return ""
>     return path.suffix.lower()
> 
> # crude classification you can refine later
> def classify_path(rel_path: str, is_dir: bool, ext: str) -> str:
>     p = rel_path.replace("\\", "/").lower()
> 
>     if is_dir:
>         return "directory"
> 
>     if p.startswith("1_evidence/01_documents"):
>         return "evidence_document"
>     if p.startswith("1_evidence/02_photos"):
>         return "evidence_photo"
>     if p.startswith("1_evidence/03_video"):
>         return "evidence_video"
>     if p.startswith("1_evidence/04_audio"):
>         return "evidence_audio"
>     if p.startswith("1_evidence/05_transcripts"):
>         return "transcript"
>     if p.startswith("1_evidence/06_exhibits_final"):
>         return "final_exhibit"
>     if p.startswith("0_case_ops/03_pleadings"):
>         return "pleading"
>     if p.startswith("0_case_ops/02_strategy"):
>         return "strategy"
>     if p.startswith("0_case_ops/01_facts"):
>         return "fact_record"
>     if p.startswith("2_data/csv"):
>         return "data_csv"
>     if p.startswith("2_data/exports"):
>         return "export"
>     if p.startswith("3_reports"):
>         return "report"
>     if p.startswith("4_governance"):
>         return "governance"
>     if p.startswith("5_scripts"):
>         return "script"
>     if p.startswith("9_archive"):
>         return "archive"
> 
>     if ext in {".md"}:
>         return "markdown"
>     if ext in {".pdf"}:
>         return "pdf"
>     if ext in {".csv"}:
>         return "csv"
>     if ext in {".xlsx", ".xls"}:
>         return "spreadsheet"
>     if ext in {".png", ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".heic", ".webp"}:
>         return "image"
>     if ext in {".mp4", ".mkv", ".mov", ".m4v"}:
>         return "video"
>     if ext in {".m4a", ".mp3", ".wav"}:
>         return "audio"
>     if ext in {".txt"}:
>         return "text"
>     if ext in {".html"}:
>         return "html"
>     if ext in {".json"}:
>         return "json"
>     if ext in {".py", ".ps1", ".bat"}:
>         return "script"
> 
>     return "unclassified"
> 
> # pull potential structured tokens from filename
> def extract_name_parts(name: str):
>     stem = Path(name).stem
> 
>     parts = {
>         "date_prefix": "",
>         "doc_id": "",
>         "issue_id": "",
>         "task_id": "",
>         "phase_id": "",
>     }
> 
>     tokens = stem.replace("—", "_").replace("-", "_").split("_")
> 
>     if len(tokens) >= 3:
>         maybe_date = "-".join(tokens[:3])
>         try:
>             datetime.strptime(maybe_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
>             parts["date_prefix"] = maybe_date
>         except Exception:
>             pass
> 
>     for token in stem.replace("—", "_").split("_"):
>         t = token.strip()
>         if t.startswith("D") and t[1:].isdigit():
>             parts["doc_id"] = t
>         elif t.startswith("ISS-"):
>             parts["issue_id"] = t
>         elif t.startswith("T-"):
>             parts["task_id"] = t
>         elif t.startswith("C-"):
>             parts["phase_id"] = t
> 
>     return parts
> 
> # ============================================================
> # MAIN
> # ============================================================
> 
> def main():
>     if not ROOT.exists():
>         raise SystemExit(f"[ERROR] Root does not exist: {ROOT}")
> 
>     OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
> 
>     rows = []
> 
>     all_paths = [ROOT] + sorted(ROOT.rglob("*"), key=lambda p: str(p).lower())
> 
>     for path in all_paths:
>         stat = safe_stat(path)
>         is_dir = path.is_dir()
> 
>         rel_path = path_to_str(path)
>         ext = get_extension(path)
>         name_parts = extract_name_parts(path.name)
> 
>         row = {
>             "manifest_created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
>             "root": str(ROOT),
>             "relative_path": rel_path,
>             "parent_relative_path": get_parent(path),
>             "name": path.name,
>             "stem": path.stem if not is_dir else path.name,
>             "suffix": ext,
>             "is_dir": is_dir,
>             "depth": get_depth(path),
>             "size_bytes": "" if is_dir or stat is None else stat.st_size,
>             "modified_at": "" if stat is None else datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime).isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
>             "created_at": "" if stat is None else datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_ctime).isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
>             "classification": classify_path(rel_path, is_dir, ext),
>             "date_prefix": name_parts["date_prefix"],
>             "doc_id": name_parts["doc_id"],
>             "issue_id": name_parts["issue_id"],
>             "task_id": name_parts["task_id"],
>             "phase_id": name_parts["phase_id"],
>             "sha256": "",
>             # control-sheet columns for later rename / move operations
>             "proposed_new_name": "",
>             "proposed_new_relative_dir": "",
>             "proposed_new_relative_path": "",
>             "action": "",          # rename | move | move_rename | keep | archive | delete
>             "notes": "",
>             "approved": "",        # yes / no
>         }
> 
>         if INCLUDE_HASH and not is_dir:
>             try:
>                 row["sha256"] = sha256_file(path)
>             except Exception as e:
>                 row["sha256"] = f"ERROR: {e}"
> 
>         rows.append(row)
> 
>     fieldnames = [
>         "manifest_created_at",
>         "root",
>         "relative_path",
>         "parent_relative_path",
>         "name",
>         "stem",
>         "suffix",
>         "is_dir",
>         "depth",
>         "size_bytes",
>         "modified_at",
>         "created_at",
>         "classification",
>         "date_prefix",
>         "doc_id",
>         "issue_id",
>         "task_id",
>         "phase_id",
>         "sha256",
>         "proposed_new_name",
>         "proposed_new_relative_dir",
>         "proposed_new_relative_path",
>         "action",
>         "notes",
>         "approved",
>     ]
> 
>     with OUTPUT_CSV.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
>         writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)
>         writer.writeheader()
>         writer.writerows(rows)
> 
>     print("=" * 90)
>     print("CONTENT MANIFEST BUILT")
>     print(f"ROOT:   {ROOT}")
>     print(f"OUTPUT: {OUTPUT_CSV}")
>     print(f"ROWS:   {len(rows)}")
>     print("=" * 90)
> 
> if __name__ == "__main__":
>     main()
> ```
> 
> Run it with:
> 
> ```powershell
> python .\5_Scripts\build_content_manifest.py
> ```
> 
> That gives you a CSV with:
> - every file and folder
> - current relative path
> - parent path
> - extension
> - size
> - timestamps
> - crude classification
> - helper columns for rename/move planning
> 
> Then the workflow is:
> 
> 1. generate manifest  
> 2. open CSV in Excel or Sheets  
> 3. fill in only these columns:
>    - `proposed_new_name`
>    - `proposed_new_relative_dir`
>    - `action`
>    - `approved`
> 4. then use a second script to execute only approved changes
> 
> Use these action meanings:
> - `keep`
> - `rename`
> - `move`
> - `move_rename`
> - `archive`
> - `delete`
> 
> Best practice: **do not edit `relative_path` manually**. That’s source truth. Put all decisions in the proposed/action columns.
> 
> When you’re ready, I’ll give you the second script that reads this CSV and safely performs the renames and moves with dry-run first.

### User
> I think also part of the reason is i have to many md files that shoudl really be rwows on a spreadsheet.

### Assistant
> Yes. That is absolutely part of the problem.
> 
> You do **not** have a file organization problem only. You have a **wrong storage type problem**.
> 
> A bunch of those `.md` files are pretending to be records. They should be rows.
> 
> Here’s the blunt rule:
> 
> **Use `.md` when the thing needs narrative, argument, explanation, or reusable prose.**  
> **Use `.csv/.xlsx` when the thing is a record, index, log, inventory, tracker, or relationship table.**
> 
> Right now you’ve got too many single-purpose note files that are really just database entries wearing a markdown costume.
> 
> ### What should stay as `.md`
> Keep markdown for:
> - motions
> - declarations
> - complaint narratives
> - legal memos
> - hearing outlines
> - cover letters
> - strategy docs
> - issue summaries that actually contain analysis
> - phase overviews
> - templates
> 
> Those are documents.
> 
> ### What should become spreadsheet rows
> These should mostly be rows, not individual `.md` files:
> - actors / people / entities
> - damages ledger entries
> - deadlines
> - docket entries
> - events
> - issues inventory
> - exhibits index
> - document inventory
> - service log
> - evidence intake log
> - task spine
> - regulator contact list
> - filing tracker
> - authority / case citation inventory
> - document-to-issue links
> - event-to-document links
> - exhibit-to-document links
> 
> Those are records.
> 
> ### Your biggest offenders from what you showed
> The obvious places where you likely have too many md files:
> - `01_Facts/Issues`
> - `01_Facts/Events`
> - portions of `Phase_Maps/*/Tasks`
> - lots of manifests and excerpt files that are basically indices
> - anything that is one note per tiny claim with mostly metadata and 3–8 lines of content
> 
> That explodes cognitive clutter and makes renaming hell.
> 
> ### Better model
> Use a hybrid:
> 
> - **Spreadsheet = master structured data**
> - **Markdown = narrative / argument / synthesis**
> - **Files in evidence folders = source artifacts**
> - **Markdown docs link back to row IDs**
> - **Rows point to source files**
> 
> So instead of 50 tiny issue notes, you could have:
> 
> - `2_Data/csv/issues.csv`
> - `2_Data/csv/events.csv`
> - `2_Data/csv/docs.csv`
> - `2_Data/csv/damages.csv`
> - `2_Data/csv/tasks.csv`
> - link tables like:
>   - `event_doc.csv`
>   - `issue_doc.csv`
>   - `issue_event.csv`
> 
> Then keep only a smaller set of markdown files for:
> - major issue memos
> - legal theories
> - argument-ready summaries
> - regulator narratives
> - hearing prep
> 
> ### Practical decision test
> For each `.md`, ask:
> 
> **Is this mostly:**
> - a single record with fields? → row
> - a list item in a bigger set? → row
> - a tracker entry? → row
> - a relationship between two things? → row in a join table
> - real prose someone might read top-to-bottom? → md
> 
> If the file would work better as one line in a table, it should not be its own note.
> 
> ### What I’d convert first
> Do this in order:
> 
> 1. **Issues**
>    - one spreadsheet
>    - keep only true issue memos as `.md`
> 
> 2. **Events**
>    - one spreadsheet
>    - only special event narratives stay as `.md`
> 
> 3. **Damages**
>    - definitely a spreadsheet ledger
> 
> 4. **Docs / exhibits**
>    - one master index spreadsheet
> 
> 5. **Tasks / deadlines**
>    - one operational tracker
> 
> That alone will cut the vault noise hard.
> 
> ### Clean target structure
> Something closer to this:
> 
> - `0_Case_Ops` = dashboards, strategy, pleadings, reference prose
> - `1_Evidence` = actual source artifacts
> - `2_Data/csv` = structured master tables
> - `3_reports` = generated summaries, exports, audits
> - `4_Governance` = schema/rules/templates
> - `5_Scripts` = automation
> - `9_Archive` = junk, deprecated, temp, raw dumps
> 
> That’s sane.
> 
> ### Recommendation
> Do **not** try to rename everything first.
> 
> First:
> - build full manifest
> - classify every file as `document` or `record`
> - identify markdown files that should be converted to rows
> - migrate the structured stuff into CSV masters
> - then rename and relocate what remains
> 
> Otherwise you’re just reorganizing the same mess into prettier mess.
> 
> Next move: I can give you a **classification script** that scans the manifest and flags likely `.md` files that should become spreadsheet rows.

### User
> can you write a script to do somethign like this to essential transfer things over to a database . use the front matter at the columens, and the page content in the body. or somethign and maybe we start by the type of records first ot match our tables.

### Assistant
> Yes. And the smart way is **not** to force perfect normalization on day one.
> 
> Start with a **staging import** that:
> 
> - scans all `.md` files
> - reads YAML front matter
> - keeps the page body as `body_md`
> - infers a `record_type`
> - routes records into the closest matching table
> - keeps all unmapped front matter in `extra_json`
> 
> That gets your vault into SQLite **without losing anything**, and then you can tighten the schema later instead of playing folder-Jenga forever.
> 
> Use this as **phase 1 importer**:
> 
> ```python
> #!/usr/bin/env python3
> """
> ingest_markdown_to_sqlite.py
> 
> Phase 1 vault-to-database importer for the FCFCU case vault.
> 
> What it does:
> - Recursively scans a vault/content folder for .md files
> - Parses YAML front matter
> - Stores markdown body as body_md
> - Infers record_type from front matter and/or path
> - Routes records into normalized starter tables:
>     actors, events, issues, damages, deadlines, docs, tasks, generic_notes
> - Keeps full front matter in raw_frontmatter_json
> - Keeps unmapped fields in extra_json
> - Tracks source file path and modified time for re-ingest/upsert
> 
> This is a staging-first design:
> - good enough to get structured data out of the vault
> - safe enough to avoid losing metadata
> - flexible enough to refine later
> 
> Requirements:
>     pip install pyyaml
> 
> Usage:
>     python ingest_markdown_to_sqlite.py ^
>       --root "g:\\My Drive\\QiVault\\3_projects\\3.3_cases\\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\\content" ^
>       --db   "g:\\My Drive\\QiVault\\3_projects\\3.3_cases\\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\\content\\2_Data\\fcfcu_case.db" ^
>       --dry-run
> 
>     python ingest_markdown_to_sqlite.py ^
>       --root "g:\\My Drive\\QiVault\\3_projects\\3.3_cases\\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\\content" ^
>       --db   "g:\\My Drive\\QiVault\\3_projects\\3.3_cases\\30.06_CASE_FCFCU\\content\\2_Data\\fcfcu_case.db"
> """
> 
> from __future__ import annotations
> 
> import argparse
> import datetime as dt
> import json
> import os
> import re
> import sqlite3
> from pathlib import Path
> from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
> 
> import yaml
> 
> 
> # =========================
> # CONFIG
> # =========================
> 
> MD_EXTENSIONS = {".md", ".markdown"}
> 
> IGNORE_DIR_NAMES = {
>     ".obsidian",
>     "9_Archive",
>     "__pycache__",
> }
> 
> IGNORE_FILE_NAMES = {
>     "_docs.md",
>     "_data.md",
>     "_scripts.md",
>     "_references.md",
> }
> 
> # Alias maps by target table.
> # Add to these as you discover more field names in your notes.
> FIELD_ALIASES = {
>     "actors": {
>         "record_id": ["record_id", "id", "actor_id", "person_id", "entity_id", "uid", "dna_id"],
>         "name": ["name", "title", "actor", "person", "entity", "full_name"],
>         "role": ["role", "capacity", "actor_role", "entity_role"],
>         "organization": ["organization", "org", "company", "institution"],
>         "email": ["email", "email_address"],
>         "phone": ["phone", "phone_number", "telephone"],
>         "status": ["status", "state"],
>         "tags": ["tags", "keywords"],
>         "created_at": ["created_at", "date_created"],
>         "updated_at": ["updated_at", "date_updated", "modified_at"],
>     },
>     "events": {
>         "record_id": ["record_id", "id", "event_id", "uid", "dna_id"],
>         "event_date": ["event_date", "date", "occurred_on", "event_dt"],
>         "title": ["title", "name", "event", "subject"],
>         "status": ["status", "state"],
>         "phase": ["phase", "phase_id", "lane", "lane_id"],
>         "related_issue_ids": ["related_issue_ids", "issue_ids", "issues"],
>         "related_doc_ids": ["related_doc_ids", "doc_ids", "documents", "related_documents"],
>         "tags": ["tags", "keywords"],
>         "created_at": ["created_at", "date_created"],
>         "updated_at": ["updated_at", "date_updated", "modified_at"],
>     },
>     "issues": {
>         "record_id": ["record_id", "id", "issue_id", "claim_id", "uid", "dna_id"],
>         "title": ["title", "name", "issue", "claim"],
>         "issue_code": ["issue_code", "code", "issue_number"],
>         "severity": ["severity", "priority"],
>         "status": ["status", "state"],
>         "phase": ["phase", "phase_id", "lane", "lane_id"],
>         "theory": ["theory", "legal_theory"],
>         "tags": ["tags", "keywords"],
>         "created_at": ["created_at", "date_created"],
>         "updated_at": ["updated_at", "date_updated", "modified_at"],
>     },
>     "damages": {
>         "record_id": ["record_id", "id", "damage_id", "uid", "dna_id"],
>         "damage_date": ["damage_date", "date", "incurred_on"],
>         "title": ["title", "name", "damage", "item"],
>         "amount": ["amount", "value", "estimated_amount"],
>         "status": ["status", "state"],
>         "category": ["category", "damage_type"],
>         "related_issue_ids": ["related_issue_ids", "issue_ids", "issues"],
>         "tags": ["tags", "keywords"],
>         "created_at": ["created_at", "date_created"],
>         "updated_at": ["updated_at", "date_updated", "modified_at"],
>     },
>     "deadlines": {
>         "record_id": ["record_id", "id", "deadline_id", "uid", "dna_id"],
>         "deadline_date": ["deadline_date", "date", "due_date", "deadline"],
>         "title": ["title", "name", "deadline", "subject"],
>         "status": ["status", "state"],
>         "phase": ["phase", "phase_id", "lane", "lane_id"],
>         "related_task_ids": ["related_task_ids", "task_ids", "tasks"],
>         "tags": ["tags", "keywords"],
>         "created_at": ["created_at", "date_created"],
>         "updated_at": ["updated_at", "date_updated", "modified_at"],
>     },
>     "docs": {
>         "record_id": ["record_id", "id", "doc_id", "document_id", "uid", "dna_id"],
>         "doc_date": ["doc_date", "date", "document_date"],
>         "title": ["title", "name", "document", "subject"],
>         "doc_type": ["doc_type", "document_type", "type", "category"],
>         "status": ["status", "state"],
>         "phase": ["phase", "phase_id", "lane", "lane_id"],
>         "related_issue_ids": ["related_issue_ids", "issue_ids", "issues"],
>         "related_event_ids": ["related_event_ids", "event_ids", "events"],
>         "tags": ["tags", "keywords"],
>         "created_at": ["created_at", "date_created"],
>         "updated_at": ["updated_at", "date_updated", "modified_at"],
>     },
>     "tasks": {
>         "record_id": ["record_id", "id", "task_id", "uid", "dna_id"],
>         "task_date": ["task_date", "date", "due_date"],
>         "title": ["title", "name", "task", "subject"],
>         "status": ["status", "state"],
>         "priority": ["priority", "severity"],
>         "phase": ["phase", "phase_id", "lane", "lane_id"],
>         "assigned_to": ["assigned_to", "owner", "responsible"],
>         "related_issue_ids": ["related_issue_ids", "issue_ids", "issues"],
>         "related_doc_ids": ["related_doc_ids", "doc_ids", "documents"],
>         "tags": ["tags", "keywords"],
>         "created_at": ["created_at", "date_created"],
>         "updated_at": ["updated_at", "date_updated", "modified_at"],
>     },
> }
> 
> RECORD_TYPE_HINTS = {
>     "actors": [
>         "actors",
>         "actor",
>         "people",
>         "person",
>         "entities",
>         "contacts",
>     ],
>     "events": [
>         "events",
>         "event",
>         "timeline",
>         "case_log",
>     ],
>     "issues": [
>         "issues",
>         "issue",
>         "claims",
>         "claim",
>         "violation",
>         "violations",
>     ],
>     "damages": [
>         "damages",
>         "damage",
>         "loss",
>         "losses",
>     ],
>     "deadlines": [
>         "deadlines",
>         "deadline",
>         "calendar",
>         "due",
>     ],
>     "docs": [
>         "docs",
>         "document",
>         "documents",
>         "filing",
>         "filings",
>         "pleading",
>         "pleadings",
>         "exhibit",
>         "exhibits",
>         "declaration",
>         "declarations",
>         "motion",
>         "motions",
>         "notice",
>         "notices",
>         "order",
>         "orders",
>         "affidavit",
>         "affidavits",
>         "complaint",
>         "complaints",
>         "memo",
>         "memorandum",
>         "brief",
>         "briefs",
>         "letter",
>         "letters",
>         "narrative",
>         "manifest",
>     ],
>     "tasks": [
>         "tasks",
>         "task",
>         "todo",
>         "todos",
>         "checklist",
>         "action",
>         "actions",
>     ],
> }
> 
> DATE_IN_NAME_RE = re.compile(r"(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})")
> FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(r"^---\s*\n(.*?)\n---\s*\n?(.*)$", re.DOTALL)
> 
> 
> # =========================
> # HELPERS
> # =========================
> 
> def safe_json(value: Any) -> str:
>     return json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, default=str, sort_keys=True)
> 
> 
> def normalize_to_list(value: Any) -> Optional[str]:
>     if value is None:
>         return None
>     if isinstance(value, list):
>         return safe_json(value)
>     if isinstance(value, (dict, tuple, set)):
>         return safe_json(value)
>     return str(value)
> 
> 
> def normalize_scalar(value: Any) -> Optional[str]:
>     if value is None:
>         return None
>     if isinstance(value, (list, dict, tuple, set)):
>         return safe_json(value)
>     return str(value)
> 
> 
> def parse_markdown_file(path: Path) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], str]:
>     text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
>     match = FRONTMATTER_RE.match(text)
> 
>     if not match:
>         return {}, text.strip()
> 
>     raw_frontmatter = match.group(1)
>     body = match.group(2).strip()
> 
>     try:
>         fm = yaml.safe_load(raw_frontmatter) or {}
>         if not isinstance(fm, dict):
>             fm = {"_frontmatter_raw": fm}
>     except Exception as exc:
>         fm = {
>             "_frontmatter_parse_error": str(exc),
>             "_frontmatter_raw_text": raw_frontmatter,
>         }
> 
>     return fm, body
> 
> 
> def infer_record_type(path: Path, frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
>     candidates = []
> 
>     for key in ["record_type", "type", "note_type", "table", "kind", "category"]:
>         if key in frontmatter and frontmatter[key]:
>             candidates.append(str(frontmatter[key]).strip().lower())
> 
>     path_parts = [p.lower() for p in path.parts]
>     file_name = path.name.lower()
>     candidates.extend(path_parts)
>     candidates.append(file_name)
> 
>     for record_type, hints in RECORD_TYPE_HINTS.items():
>         for candidate in candidates:
>             if any(hint in candidate for hint in hints):
>                 return record_type
> 
>     return "generic_notes"
> 
> 
> def first_matching_value(frontmatter: Dict[str, Any], aliases: List[str]) -> Any:
>     lowered = {str(k).lower(): v for k, v in frontmatter.items()}
>     for alias in aliases:
>         if alias.lower() in lowered:
>             return lowered[alias.lower()]
>     return None
> 
> 
> def extract_mapped_fields(table_name: str, frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], Dict[str, Any]]:
>     alias_map = FIELD_ALIASES.get(table_name, {})
>     mapped: Dict[str, Any] = {}
>     consumed_keys = set()
> 
>     lower_to_original = {str(k).lower(): k for k in frontmatter.keys()}
> 
>     for canonical_field, aliases in alias_map.items():
>         value = first_matching_value(frontmatter, aliases)
>         if value is not None:
>             mapped[canonical_field] = value
>             for alias in aliases:
>                 alias_l = alias.lower()
>                 if alias_l in lower_to_original:
>                     consumed_keys.add(lower_to_original[alias_l])
> 
>     extra = {k: v for k, v in frontmatter.items() if k not in consumed_keys}
>     return mapped, extra
> 
> 
> def infer_title(path: Path, frontmatter: Dict[str, Any], body: str) -> str:
>     for key in ["title", "name", "subject"]:
>         if frontmatter.get(key):
>             return str(frontmatter[key]).strip()
> 
>     stem = path.stem
>     stem = stem.replace("_", " ").replace("-", " ").strip()
> 
>     first_heading = None
>     for line in body.splitlines():
>         if line.strip().startswith("#"):
>             first_heading = line.strip().lstrip("#").strip()
>             break
> 
>     if first_heading:
>         return first_heading
> 
>     return stem
> 
> 
> def infer_date_from_frontmatter_or_name(path: Path, frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
>     for key in [
>         "date",
>         "event_date",
>         "doc_date",
>         "damage_date",
>         "deadline_date",
>         "task_date",
>         "created_at",
>     ]:
>         if frontmatter.get(key):
>             return str(frontmatter[key])
> 
>     m = DATE_IN_NAME_RE.search(path.name)
>     if m:
>         return m.group(1)
> 
>     return None
> 
> 
> def should_skip(path: Path) -> bool:
>     if path.suffix.lower() not in MD_EXTENSIONS:
>         return True
> 
>     if path.name in IGNORE_FILE_NAMES:
>         return True
> 
>     for part in path.parts:
>         if part in IGNORE_DIR_NAMES:
>             return True
> 
>     return False
> 
> 
> # =========================
> # DB SETUP
> # =========================
> 
> def create_tables(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> None:
>     cur = conn.cursor()
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS source_files (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         file_name TEXT,
>         parent_dir TEXT,
>         record_type TEXT,
>         modified_at TEXT,
>         imported_at TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS actors (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         record_id TEXT,
>         name TEXT,
>         role TEXT,
>         organization TEXT,
>         email TEXT,
>         phone TEXT,
>         status TEXT,
>         tags TEXT,
>         created_at TEXT,
>         updated_at TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         extra_json TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         record_id TEXT,
>         event_date TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         status TEXT,
>         phase TEXT,
>         related_issue_ids TEXT,
>         related_doc_ids TEXT,
>         tags TEXT,
>         created_at TEXT,
>         updated_at TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         extra_json TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         record_id TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         issue_code TEXT,
>         severity TEXT,
>         status TEXT,
>         phase TEXT,
>         theory TEXT,
>         tags TEXT,
>         created_at TEXT,
>         updated_at TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         extra_json TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS damages (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         record_id TEXT,
>         damage_date TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         amount TEXT,
>         status TEXT,
>         category TEXT,
>         related_issue_ids TEXT,
>         tags TEXT,
>         created_at TEXT,
>         updated_at TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         extra_json TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS deadlines (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         record_id TEXT,
>         deadline_date TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         status TEXT,
>         phase TEXT,
>         related_task_ids TEXT,
>         tags TEXT,
>         created_at TEXT,
>         updated_at TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         extra_json TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS docs (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         record_id TEXT,
>         doc_date TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         doc_type TEXT,
>         status TEXT,
>         phase TEXT,
>         related_issue_ids TEXT,
>         related_event_ids TEXT,
>         tags TEXT,
>         created_at TEXT,
>         updated_at TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         extra_json TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         record_id TEXT,
>         task_date TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         status TEXT,
>         priority TEXT,
>         phase TEXT,
>         assigned_to TEXT,
>         related_issue_ids TEXT,
>         related_doc_ids TEXT,
>         tags TEXT,
>         created_at TEXT,
>         updated_at TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         extra_json TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     cur.execute("""
>     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS generic_notes (
>         source_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
>         record_id TEXT,
>         title TEXT,
>         inferred_date TEXT,
>         record_type TEXT,
>         status TEXT,
>         tags TEXT,
>         body_md TEXT,
>         raw_frontmatter_json TEXT,
>         extra_json TEXT
>     )
>     """)
> 
>     conn.commit()
> 
> 
> # =========================
> # UPSERTS
> # =========================
> 
> def upsert_source_file(
>     conn: sqlite3.Connection,
>     source_path: str,
>     file_name: str,
>     parent_dir: str,
>     record_type: str,
>     modified_at: str,
>     imported_at: str,
>     title: str,
>     raw_frontmatter_json: str,
>     body_md: str,
> ) -> None:
>     conn.execute("""
>     INSERT INTO source_files (
>         source_path, file_name, parent_dir, record_type, modified_at, imported_at,
>         title, raw_frontmatter_json, body_md
>     )
>     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
>     ON CONFLICT(source_path) DO UPDATE SET
>         file_name=excluded.file_name,
>         parent_dir=excluded.parent_dir,
>         record_type=excluded.record_type,
>         modified_at=excluded.modified_at,
>         imported_at=excluded.imported_at,
>         title=excluded.title,
>         raw_frontmatter_json=excluded.raw_frontmatter_json,
>         body_md=excluded.body_md
>     """, (
>         source_path, file_name, parent_dir, record_type, modified_at, imported_at,
>         title, raw_frontmatter_json, body_md
>     ))
> 
> 
> def upsert_table(conn: sqlite3.Connection, table_name: str, row: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
>     cols = list(row.keys())
>     placeholders = ", ".join(["?"] * len(cols))
>     updates = ", ".join([f"{c}=excluded.{c}" for c in cols if c != "source_path"])
>     sql = f"""
>     INSERT INTO {table_name} ({", ".join(cols)})
>     VALUES ({placeholders})
>     ON CONFLICT(source_path) DO UPDATE SET
>         {updates}
>     """
>     values = [row[c] for c in cols]
>     conn.execute(sql, values)
> 
> 
> def route_record(
>     conn: sqlite3.Connection,
>     record_type: str,
>     source_path: str,
>     title: str,
>     inferred_date: Optional[str],
>     frontmatter: Dict[str, Any],
>     body: str,
> ) -> None:
>     raw_frontmatter_json = safe_json(frontmatter)
> 
>     if record_type in FIELD_ALIASES:
>         mapped, extra = extract_mapped_fields(record_type, frontmatter)
> 
>         row = {
>             "source_path": source_path,
>             "body_md": body,
>             "raw_frontmatter_json": raw_frontmatter_json,
>             "extra_json": safe_json(extra),
>         }
> 
>         for key, value in mapped.items():
>             if key in {"tags", "related_issue_ids", "related_doc_ids", "related_event_ids", "related_task_ids"}:
>                 row[key] = normalize_to_list(value)
>             else:
>                 row[key] = normalize_scalar(value)
> 
>         if "title" not in row or not row.get("title"):
>             row["title"] = title
> 
>         # table-specific fallback dates
>         if record_type == "events" and not row.get("event_date"):
>             row["event_date"] = inferred_date
>         elif record_type == "damages" and not row.get("damage_date"):
>             row["damage_date"] = inferred_date
>         elif record_type == "deadlines" and not row.get("deadline_date"):
>             row["deadline_date"] = inferred_date
>         elif record_type == "docs" and not row.get("doc_date"):
>             row["doc_date"] = inferred_date
>         elif record_type == "tasks" and not row.get("task_date"):
>             row["task_date"] = inferred_date
> 
>         upsert_table(conn, record_type, row)
>         return
> 
>     # fallback
>     generic_row = {
>         "source_path": source_path,
>         "record_id": normalize_scalar(
>             frontmatter.get("record_id")
>             or frontmatter.get("id")
>             or frontmatter.get("uid")
>             or frontmatter.get("dna_id")
>         ),
>         "title": title,
>         "inferred_date": inferred_date,
>         "record_type": record_type,
>         "status": normalize_scalar(frontmatter.get("status")),
>         "tags": normalize_to_list(frontmatter.get("tags")),
>         "body_md": body,
>         "raw_frontmatter_json": raw_frontmatter_json,
>         "extra_json": raw_frontmatter_json,
>     }
>     upsert_table(conn, "generic_notes", generic_row)
> 
> 
> # =========================
> # MAIN INGEST
> # =========================
> 
> def scan_markdown_files(root: Path) -> List[Path]:
>     results: List[Path] = []
>     for path in root.rglob("*"):
>         if path.is_file() and not should_skip(path):
>             results.append(path)
>     return sorted(results)
> 
> 
> def ingest(root: Path, db_path: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> None:
>     files = scan_markdown_files(root)
> 
>     print("=" * 96)
>     print("VAULT TO SQLITE IMPORT")
>     print(f"ROOT     = {root}")
>     print(f"DB       = {db_path}")
>     print(f"DRY_RUN  = {dry_run}")
>     print(f"FILES    = {len(files)}")
>     print("=" * 96)
> 
>     conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))
>     conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
> 
>     try:
>         create_tables(conn)
> 
>         imported_at = dt.datetime.now().isoformat(timespec="seconds")
>         counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
> 
>         for idx, path in enumerate(files, start=1):
>             rel_path = path.relative_to(root)
>             frontmatter, body = parse_markdown_file(path)
>             record_type = infer_record_type(path, frontmatter)
>             title = infer_title(path, frontmatter, body)
>             inferred_date = infer_date_from_frontmatter_or_name(path, frontmatter)
>             modified_at = dt.datetime.fromtimestamp(path.stat().st_mtime).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
> 
>             counts[record_type] = counts.get(record_type, 0) + 1
> 
>             print(f"[{idx:04d}/{len(files):04d}] {record_type:<14} {rel_path}")
> 
>             if dry_run:
>                 continue
> 
>             upsert_source_file(
>                 conn=conn,
>                 source_path=str(rel_path).replace("\\", "/"),
>                 file_name=path.name,
>                 parent_dir=str(rel_path.parent).replace("\\", "/"),
>                 record_type=record_type,
>                 modified_at=modified_at,
>                 imported_at=imported_at,
>                 title=title,
>                 raw_frontmatter_json=safe_json(frontmatter),
>                 body_md=body,
>             )
> 
>             route_record(
>                 conn=conn,
>                 record_type=record_type,
>                 source_path=str(rel_path).replace("\\", "/"),
>                 title=title,
>                 inferred_date=inferred_date,
>                 frontmatter=frontmatter,
>                 body=body,
>             )
> 
>         if not dry_run:
>             conn.commit()
> 
>         print("\nSUMMARY")
>         print("-" * 96)
>         for key in sorted(counts):
>             print(f"{key:<14} {counts[key]:>6}")
>         print("-" * 96)
> 
>         if dry_run:
>             print("[DRY RUN] No database changes written.")
>         else:
>             print(f"[OK] Import complete: {db_path}")
> 
>     finally:
>         conn.close()
> 
> 
> # =========================
> # CLI
> # =========================
> 
> def main() -> None:
>     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
>     parser.add_argument("--root", required=True, help="Path to vault/content root")
>     parser.add_argument("--db", required=True, help="Path to sqlite database file")
>     parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Scan and classify only, do not write")
>     args = parser.parse_args()
> 
>     root = Path(args.root)
>     db_path = Path(args.db)
> 
>     if not root.exists() or not root.is_dir():
>         raise SystemExit(f"[ERROR] Root directory does not exist or is not a directory: {root}")
> 
>     db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
> 
>     ingest(root=root, db_path=db_path, dry_run=args.dry_run)
> 
> 
> if __name__ == "__main__":
>     main()
> ```
>

> - `events`
> - `issues`
> - `damages`
> - `deadlines`
> - `docs`
> - `tasks`
> - `generic_notes`

> - route obvious records into starter tables
> - leave weird stuff in `generic_notes`
> 
> That keeps you from overengineering yourself into paralysis.

### User
> i dont understand when i link the table no new field shows up on the other table.

### Assistant
> Because a **link/relationship field is not the same thing as mirroring all fields** from the other table.
> 
> What usually happens:
> 
> - You create a relation from **Table A** to **Table B**
> - That only gives you a **pointer** to the record in Table B
> - It does **not automatically copy** name, status, date, amount, etc. into Table A
> 
> So if you linked, say, an **Event** to an **Issue**, the Event table will only show the linked Issue record unless you also add:
> 
> - a **lookup / rollup / property mirror**
> - or a **reciprocal relation field**
> - or a **formula based on the relation**, depending on the app
> 
> ### The core distinction
> There are 3 different things people mix up:
> 
> **1. Relation / Link**  
> Just connects records.
> 
> **2. Backlink / Reciprocal relation**  
> Shows the connection on the other table too.
> 
> **3. Lookup / Rollup**  
> Actually pulls another field across, like Issue Status, Owner, or Phase.
> 
> ### Why you’re not seeing “new fields”
> Because most systems do **not** auto-create mirrored columns for every linked table field.  
> That would be chaos and duplicate data.
> 
> Instead, you usually have to explicitly add:
> - “Show related records from X”
> - “Lookup field from linked record”
> - “Rollup linked property”
> - “Mirror property”
> 
> ### Example
> If:
> 
> - `events` has `related_issue`
> - `issues` has fields: `title`, `severity`, `status`
> 
> Then linking an event to an issue does **not** automatically create:
> - issue severity
> - issue status
> - issue title columns in events
> 
> You must add lookup columns like:
> - `issue_title`
> - `issue_status`
> - `issue_severity`
> 
> ### If what you wanted was bidirectional linking
> Then you need the relation to be set as:
> - **two-way / reciprocal / show on both tables**
> 
> Otherwise it may only exist from one side.
> 
> ### If what you wanted was synced fields
> Then you need:
> - relation first
> - then lookup/rollup fields pulling from that relation
> 
> ### Blunt version
> You’re expecting a **foreign key** to behave like a **joined view**.  
> It won’t. The relation is just the key, not the whole joined record.
> 
> If you tell me **what app this is in** — Capacities, Notion, Airtable, Obsidian DB plugin, Coda, or something else — I’ll tell you the exact clicks and what field type you actually need.

### User
> ok so i created all the tables in noco

### Assistant
> Good. Then here’s the clean mental model for **NocoDB**:
> 
> ## What you created
> Each table is its own thing:
> - `events`
> - `issues`
> - `docs`
> - `claims`
> - `filings`
> - etc.
> 
> When you add a **Link to another record** field, NocoDB creates a relationship, not a field copy.
> 
> ## Why you don’t see fields from the other table
> Because NocoDB does **not** automatically expose all columns from the linked table inside the current table.
> 
> A link field only stores the connection.
> 
> So:
> 
> - `events.related_issue -> issues`
> - means the event knows which issue it points to
> - but it does **not** automatically show `issue_code`, `severity`, `status`, etc.
> 
> ## What you need in NocoDB
> You usually need **3 layers**:
> 
> ### 1. Link field
> Example in `events`:
> - `related_issue` → links to `issues`
> 
> ### 2. Back relation
> NocoDB often gives you the reverse relationship automatically on the other table.
> Example in `issues`:
> - `events` → shows all linked events
> 
> That reverse field is not a copied column either. It’s just the relationship from the other side.
> 
> ### 3. Lookup field
> If you want data from the linked record to show in the current table, add a **Lookup**.
> 
> Example:
> In `events`, after linking `related_issue`:
> - `issue_code_lookup`
> - `issue_status_lookup`
> - `issue_severity_lookup`
> 
> Those pull values from the linked `issues` record.
> 
> ## Simple example
> 
> ### Table: `issues`
> Columns:
> - `id`
> - `issue_code`
> - `title`
> - `status`
> - `severity`
> 
> ### Table: `events`
> Columns:
> - `id`
> - `event_date`
> - `title`
> - `related_issue` ← link to `issues`
> - `issue_code_lookup` ← lookup from `related_issue.issue_code`
> - `issue_status_lookup` ← lookup from `related_issue.status`
> 
> That’s the part you were missing.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Important reality check
> You do **not** want every field mirrored everywhere. That gets stupid fast.
> 
> Use links for:
> - relationships
> - record navigation
> - many-to-many structure
> 
> Use lookups only for:
> - fields you actually need visible in that table view
> 
> Otherwise you’ll build spreadsheet spaghetti in a database suit.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Best structure for your case
> For your litigation setup, I’d do this:
> 
> ### Core tables
> - `actors`
> - `events`
> - `issues`
> - `docs`
> - `claims`
> - `filings`
> - `exhibits`
> - `tasks`
> - `phases`
> 
> ### Join tables for many-to-many
> Do **not** cram many-to-many into one ugly multiselect field if you want this clean.
> 
> Use bridge tables like:
> - `event_issue`
> - `event_doc`
> - `doc_claim`
> - `task_doc`
> - `task_event`
> 
> That matches your governance templates and keeps the system sane.
> 
> Example:
> 
> #### `event_issue`
> - `id`
> - `event_id` → link to `events`
> - `issue_id` → link to `issues`
> - `relation_type`
> - `notes`
> 
> Then you can lookup:
> - event title
> - issue code
> - issue severity
> 
> without bloating the main tables.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Why this matters
> Because some of your markdown files are really:
> - one row in `issues`
> - one row in `events`
> - one row in `docs`
> - one row in `claims`
> 
> and some are actually:
> - narrative memos
> - working notes
> - packets
> - legal prose
> 
> Do **not** dump all markdown into one giant “docs” bucket and call it architecture. That’s fake organization.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Your next move
> Do this in order:
> 
> ### Option A: database-first
> 1. Finalize your core tables
> 2. Add link fields
> 3. Add only the lookup fields you actually need
> 4. Add join tables for many-to-many
> 5. Then import CSV rows
> 
> ### Option B: markdown-to-db migration
> 1. Build a content manifest CSV
> 2. Classify each file as:
>    - row record
>    - attachment/source
>    - narrative memo
>    - archive/junk
> 3. Import only the row-record files into Noco
> 4. Leave narrative docs in markdown and link them by path/id
> 
> This is the smarter route for your vault.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Concrete answer to your exact problem in Noco
> If you linked two tables and expected fields to appear:
> 
> 1. Open the table where you made the link
> 2. Add a new field
> 3. Choose **Lookup**
> 4. Select the relation field you already created
> 5. Select the target field from the linked table
> 
> That’s how you pull the data through.
> 
> If you want, send me the tables you made in Noco and I’ll map:
> - which should be direct tables
> - which should be join tables
> - which fields should be links
> - which fields should be lookups
> - which markdown files should stay out of the database entirely

### Assistant
> Yes. If the MCP connection is live, your IDE can absolutely **read, create, and update records** in NocoDB through those exposed tools. Your artifact shows the base is real, the tables are real, and the server is exposing create/update/delete actions, not just read-only access. fileciteturn2file0
> 
> What that means in plain English:
> 
> - `getTablesList` / `getTableSchema` = the IDE can inspect structure
> - `queryRecords` / `getRecord` = it can read data
> - `createRecords` = it can add rows
> - `updateRecords` = it can edit rows
> - `deleteRecords` = it can remove rows
> 
> So yes, the IDE can make entries and maintain tables **as long as**:
> - the MCP server stays connected
> - the credentials/token used by MCP still have write permission
> - the IDE agent is actually configured to call those MCP tools, not just “see” them
> 
> Now the more important part: your schema confirms the database side is viable, but it also confirms why your file system feels bloated.
> 
> ## What the artifact tells us
> 
> You now have proper core tables for:
> - cases
> - documents
> - actors
> - events
> - filings
> - issues
> - damages
> - deadlines
> - facts
> 
> That is enough to start moving the “fake row markdown” out of `.md` hell and into actual records. fileciteturn2file0
> 
> ## Blunt truth
> 
> A lot of your markdown should not stay markdown.
> 
> These should mostly become rows:
> - actors
> - events
> - issues
> - damages
> - deadlines
> - filings
> - documents metadata
> - facts
> 
> These should mostly stay markdown:
> - legal memos
> - narrative summaries
> - hearing strategy
> - regulator packet drafts
> - argument outlines
> - cover letters
> - long-form analysis
> 
> That split is your cleanup key.
> 
> ## One schema warning
> 
> Your current Noco schema is workable, but not clean yet.
> 
> A few things jump out:
> 
> - `cases` has lots of direct link fields to nearly every table. That’s normal, but you do **not** need to manually populate both sides if the relationship is already linked through the related table.
> - `documents` is clearly your main registry table and should probably become the central ingest target first. fileciteturn2file0
> - `issues` links to `theory` and `statute`, which is good, but that means your importer should not guess those unless you already have a mapping. fileciteturn2file0
> - `damages` and `deadlines` already have enough structure to stop using random md notes as trackers. fileciteturn2file0
> - `facts` is too thin right now. It only has title plus links. You’ll probably want fields like `fact_text`, `source_doc`, `confidence`, `fact_date`, maybe `fact_type`. fileciteturn2file0
> 
> ## Best import order
> 
> Do **not** try to import everything at once. That’s how you create garbage fast.
> 
> Do it in this order:
> 
> 1. `cases`
> 2. `actors`
> 3. `documents`
> 4. `events`
> 5. `filings`
> 6. `issues`
> 7. `damages`
> 8. `deadlines`
> 9. `facts`
> 
> Why this order:
> - everything links back to the case
> - actors and documents are foundational
> - events/filings/issues/damages/deadlines depend on those
> - facts should come last because they are the most granular and easiest to overproduce
> 
> ## Your next move
> 
> You need a **local export/import script pipeline**, not more manual clicking.
> 
> I’d build this in two stages:
> 
> ### Stage 1: export manifest from your content folder
> Script outputs CSV with:
> - source_path
> - filename
> - extension
> - inferred_record_type
> - frontmatter_json
> - body_preview
> - suggested_table
> - suggested_title
> - suggested_case
> - status
> 
> ### Stage 2: import selected rows into Noco
> Script reads the CSV and:
> - filters by `suggested_table`
> - maps frontmatter fields to Noco columns
> - sends records through MCP or REST API
> - stores original markdown body in a long text field like `clean_text`, `summary`, `notes`, or `body_md`
> 
> ## Answer to your exact question
> 
> Yes — the MCP working means your IDE can act like a database operator now.
> 
> But don’t hand it unlimited freedom and pray.
> 
> Use it for:
> - schema reads
> - controlled row creation
> - controlled updates
> - scripted imports
> - dedupe checks
> 
> Do **not** use it yet for:
> - blind autonomous refactors
> - mass deletes
> - “figure it out” bulk linking across all tables
> 
> That’s how you end up with a beautiful disaster.
> 
> ## What I’d do next

> 
> 1. `build_content_manifest.py`
>    - scans the whole vault

>    - infers target table
>    - writes `content_manifest.csv`

> 2. `import_noco_records.py`

> Goal:
> Build a SAFE, LOCAL-FIRST Python pipeline that scans the repository, extracts markdown front matter and body content, infers a target NocoDB table, and exports a reviewable CSV manifest. Do NOT modify or move any source files yet. Do NOT archive anything. Do NOT rename anything. Do NOT delete anything.
> 
> Primary deliverable:
> Create a script named:
> 5_Scripts\build_content_manifest.py
> 
> Create output files in:
> 2_Data\exports\manifest_build\
> 
> Required outputs:
> 1. content_manifest_full.csv
> 2. content_manifest_full.jsonl
> 3. content_manifest_summary.csv
> 4. manifest_build_log.txt
> 
> Scope:
> - Recursively scan the entire content folder except:
>   - .obsidian
>   - 9_Archive
>   - node_modules
>   - __pycache__
> - Include files with these extensions:
>   - .md
>   - .pdf
>   - .txt
>   - .csv
>   - .json
>   - .html
>   - .eml
>   - .jpg
>   - .jpeg
>   - .png
>   - .heic
>   - .mp4
>   - .m4a
>   - .xlsx
> - Do not OCR anything.
> - Do not read binary contents except basic file metadata.
> - For markdown files, parse YAML front matter if present and capture the markdown body.
> - For non-markdown files, capture metadata only.
> 
> For each file, output these columns in content_manifest_full.csv:
> - record_id
> - source_path
> - relative_path
> - parent_folder
> - filename
> - stem
> - extension
> - size_bytes
> - modified_utc
> - is_markdown
> - has_frontmatter
> - frontmatter_json
> - body_text
> - body_preview
> - inferred_record_type
> - suggested_table
> - suggested_title
> - case_id_guess
> - phase_guess
> - lane_guess
> - needs_review
> - review_reason
> 
> Rules for body fields:
> - body_text only for markdown and txt files
> - body_preview limited to first 500 chars
> - preserve line breaks reasonably
> - no truncation in body_text unless file is extremely large; if over 200000 chars, store truncated body_text and mark review_reason
> 
> Inference rules:
> - Infer record type from BOTH path and filename
> - Suggested table should map to likely Noco tables such as:
>   - cases
>   - documents
>   - actors
>   - events
>   - filings
>   - issues
>   - damages
>   - deadlines
>   - facts
>   - unknown
> - Heuristics:
>   - paths containing /Actors/ => actors
>   - /Events/ => events
>   - /Issues/ => issues
>   - /Damages/ => damages
>   - /Deadlines/ => deadlines
>   - /Docket/ or court-filed pleadings PDFs => filings or documents depending on certainty
>   - evidence folders and non-row artifacts default to documents
>   - strategic memos and narrative docs default to facts or documents; if unclear mark unknown and needs_review=True
> - Never silently invent certainty. If unclear, set needs_review=True and explain why.
> 
> Also generate content_manifest_summary.csv with grouped counts by:
> - extension
> - inferred_record_type
> - suggested_table
> - parent_folder
> 
> Implementation requirements:
> - Use only Python standard library plus PyYAML if available
> - If PyYAML is unavailable, fall back to a simple front matter parser
> - Make paths Windows-safe
> - Use UTF-8 output
> - Quote CSV safely
> - Add clear logging
> - Be idempotent
> - No network calls
> - No Noco writes yet
> 
> Also create:
> 5_Scripts\README_manifest_build.md
> 
> README should explain:
> - what the script does
> - how to run it
> - what each output file means
> - that this is pre-import review only
> 
> Success condition:
> When I run the script locally, it creates the output files without changing any source repo content.
> ```
>

> Big warning: if a relation already exists, don’t sit there expecting a “magic mirrored field” unless you created the reciprocal lookup/linked display field correctly. A link creates the relationship. Extra visible fields on the other table usually need either:
> - a linked record field on both sides, or
> - a lookup/rollup field
> 
> So while the IDE runs, your real job is:
> - make sure field names are clean
> - make sure the `case` link exists on child tables
> - make sure each table has one obvious long-text field for imported note/body content
> - make sure you’re not duplicating fields with slightly different names
>

> 3. Fix `actors`
> 4. Fix `filings`
> 5. Leave the rest alone unless obviously broken

### Assistant
> Yep. I checked the manifest, and it proved your suspicion.
> 
> What it found:
> - 2,697 total records
> - 1,403 `.mp4` files
> - 618 `.md` files
> - 259 `.pdf` files
> - 277 `.jpg` files
> - 527 markdown files with front matter
> - 531 rows flagged for review
> - almost everything got dumped into `documents`
> 
> That means the manifest script worked as a filesystem inventory, but it is still too dumb for real table migration. It’s basically saying “I found the stuff,” not “I modeled the case.”
> 
> Big thing I noticed:
> - it also picked up `.trash` content, which is noise
> - it only classified **1 record as filings**
> - it treated almost all notes as generic `documents`
> 
> So your instinct was right: too many `.md` files are acting like database rows, but the current classifier is not splitting them into proper row types yet.
> 
> ## What this means
> You are **not ready to import this whole CSV into Noco** as-is.
> 
> If you do, you’ll just create one giant junk drawer table.
> 
> ## What we should do next
> We do this in phases:
> 
> ### Phase 1 — promote true row-notes into table rows
> Start with folders that are obviously structured records:
> - `0_Case_Ops/01_Facts/Actors` → `actors`
> - `0_Case_Ops/01_Facts/Events` → `events`
> - `0_Case_Ops/01_Facts/Issues` → `issues`
> - `0_Case_Ops/01_Facts/Damages` → `damages`
> - `0_Case_Ops/01_Facts/Deadlines` → `deadlines`
> - `0_Case_Ops/01_Facts/Docket` → probably `filings` or `documents`
> - `03_Pleadings/Court_Filed/...` → `filings`
> - `1_Evidence/...` → mostly `documents` / `exhibits`
> 
> ### Phase 2 — leave real artifacts as artifacts
> These should mostly stay file-backed and just become linked records:
> - PDFs
> - images
> - video
> - audio
> - spreadsheets
> - email files
> 
> ### Phase 3 — split note types properly
> A markdown note should only become a row when it is acting like:
> - one actor
> - one issue
> - one event
> - one filing
> - one damage item
> - one deadline
> - one phase
> - one task
> - one exhibit record
> 
> Everything else is reference memo, narrative, strategy note, or source doc.
> 
> ## The real problem
> Right now you have three different things mixed together in markdown:
> - row records
> - longform memos
> - file indexes / covers / manifests
> 
> Those should not import the same way.
> 
> ## Clean next move
> We write a second script that:
> 1. ignores `.trash`, `.obsidian`, and archive paths
> 2. classifies records by path first, filename second
> 3. outputs **separate CSVs per target table**
> 4. only exports row-like markdown into row tables
> 5. exports artifacts into `documents` or `exhibits`
> 
> ## Recommended first export set
> Do these first because they’re the cleanest:
> 
> - `actors_import.csv`
> - `events_import.csv`
> - `issues_import.csv`
> - `damages_import.csv`
> - `deadlines_import.csv`
> 
> Then second wave:
> - `filings_import.csv`
> - `documents_import.csv`
> - `exhibits_import.csv`
>

> ## Straight truth
> You do not have “too many markdown files” in general.
> 
> You have **too many markdown files pretending to be both documents and database rows at the same time**.
> 
> That is the actual mess.
> 
> The fix is not deleting markdown.
> The fix is **promoting row-like notes into tables and leaving narrative/source notes alone**.
> 
> I can give you the next Python script now: one that reads this manifest and generates table-specific import CSVs for `actors`, `events`, `issues`, `damages`, `deadlines`, `filings`, and `documents`.

> ```
>

> 
> ## What you should do in Noco while it works
> 
> Do these manually:
> 
> ### 1. Make the views
> For `tasks`:
> - Kanban by `status`
> - Grid sorted by `priority`, then `due_date`
> 
> For `phases`:
> - Grid sorted by `sort_order`
> 
> For `issues`:
> - Group by `theory_cluster`
> 
> For `events`:
> - Sort by `event_date`
> 
> ### 2. Clean select values
> Standardize these now so you don’t build crap taxonomy later.
> 
> **task status**
> - backlog
> - ready
> - in_progress
> - waiting
> - blocked
> - done
> - dropped
> 
> **phase status**
> - planned
> - active
> - blocked
> - complete
> - archived
> 
> **priority**
> - critical
> - high
> - normal
> - low
> 
> ### 3. Add one case row first
> Create the `cases` row now if the IDE doesn’t:
> - `FCFCU`
> - `Financial Center First Credit Union v. Cody Rice-Velasquez`
> - `49D03-2501-MF-002559`
> 
> That gives all linked tables something clean to point at.
>

> 
> ## Straight answer to your bigger idea
> 
> Yes — this is the right staging move.
> 
> You are basically building the skeleton:
> - tables
> - links
> - canonical statuses
> - strategic buckets
> 
> Then later we migrate:
> - real row-notes first
> - artifacts second
> - messy leftovers last
> 
> That is how you stop this from turning into a prettier mess.
> 
> When your IDE finishes, paste back the schema result or screenshots of the tables list and I’ll tell you exactly what’s still wrong and what to import first.

### Assistant
> Much better. This is finally **real structure** instead of soup.
> 
> But it is still carrying some chaos, and if you don’t tighten it now, the database import will inherit the mess.
> 
> ## What looks good
> 
> ### Strong moves
> - Top-level split is right:
>   - `0_Case_Ops`
>   - `1_Evidence`
>   - `2_Data`
>   - `3_reports`
>   - `4_Governance`
>   - `5_Scripts`
>   - `9_Archive`
> 
> That is clean and legible.
> 
> - `0_Case_Ops` now behaves like the **working brain**
> - `1_Evidence` behaves like the **artifact vault**
> - `2_Data` behaves like the **structured layer**
> - `4_Governance` and `5_Scripts` are properly separated
> - `9_Archive` is quarantined instead of polluting active work
> 
> That overall architecture is solid.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## What is still wrong
> 
> ### 1. `0_Case_Ops` is still too fat
> You’ve got:
> - facts
> - strategy
> - pleadings
> - references
> - drafts
> 
> That is okay conceptually, but right now it is still mixing:
> - final work product
> - rough drafts
> - reference material
> - active structured facts
> 
> That can still turn into a trash drawer.
> 
> ### 2. casing and naming are inconsistent
> You currently have all of these mixed:
> - `damages`
> - `complaint`
> - `disputes`
> - `motions`
> - `objections`
> - `due-process`
> - `foreclosure`
> - `3_reports`
> 
> Pick one style and stop drifting.
> 
> Use:
> - Title-like folder names with underscores
> - no lowercase/random hybrid drift
> - no hyphen vs underscore inconsistency unless truly needed
> 
> For example:
> - `damages` → `Damages`
> - `complaint` → `Complaints`
> - `disputes` → `Disputes`
> - `due-process` → `Due_Process`
> 
> ### 3. there are still obvious junk files in active evidence
> This stuff should not remain in active evidence:
> - `dfadadf.pdf`
> - `dfafa.pdf`
> - `dfafdsasdf.pdf`
> - `dfasdfadsffafaf.pdf`
> - `rttet.pdf`
